digests.markdown:
## Three things that matter
1. **Qualcomm's $4B Modular purchase is a software land-grab.** Chipmakers are paying up for the compiler layer to loosen Nvidia's CUDA lock-in; expect comps pressure on any semis name without a software story, and watch whether AMD responds inorganically.
2. **The IPO queue now totals ~$3.5T in target value.** SpaceX, OpenAI, and Anthropic are in registration simultaneously — an unprecedented equity-supply test. Syndicate desks reportedly split on SpaceX float size; allocation dynamics will set the tone for every deal behind it.
3. **HBM supply reportedly sold out through 2027.** Memory is the binding constraint of the AI buildout; pricing power keeps shifting to SK Hynix, Samsung, and Micron, and the export-control conversation is creeping from lithography toward advanced packaging.
## Deal flow
Adobe added Topaz Labs (terms undisclosed), its second AI-imaging tuck-in in a month. Google/Wiz remains in regulatory review with closing targeted for 2026. Nothing new on the take-private front since the EA financing syndicate priced.
## Watching
Anthropic listing-structure chatter (dual-class reports) and TSMC's June sales print as the next semis demand read. CoreWeave's 2031 converts trading above par suggests the neocloud financing window is reopening.
